title: Debugging Multi-Agent State
concept: Using central trace logs to trace issues back to a specific communication or handoff failure.

exercises.prompt: What is a key reason to switch from a monolithic agent to a multi-agent system?

options.text: To handle complex tasks more effectively

exercises.explain: Switching to a multi-agent system helps manage complex tasks by breaking them down and allowing specialized agents to communicate and collaborate.

cards.title: What is Debugging?
cards.body: Debugging is the process of finding and fixing errors in a system. In multi-agent systems, it helps identify communication or coordination issues.

cards.title: Central Trace Logs
cards.body: Central trace logs are records that document messages and actions taken by agents. They help track interactions and show where things might have gone wrong.

cards.title: Agents as Team Players
cards.body: Imagine a sports team. If one player misses a pass, the team may fail to score. Central trace logs are like the game footage that shows exactly where the pass went wrong.

cards.title: Tracing a Communication Failure
cards.body: If Agent A sends a message to Agent B but it doesn’t respond, checking the trace logs can reveal whether the message was sent successfully or lost in transit.

cards.title: Logs are Not Just for Errors
cards.body: Some believe logs are only useful for finding bugs, but they also help analyze overall system performance and communication patterns.

cards.title: Visualizing Log Data
cards.body: Central trace logs can be visualized to understand agent interactions better.

left.text: Raw log data can be overwhelming.

right.text: Visualized data shows clear patterns and issues.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary use of central trace logs in a multi-agent system?

options.text: To track agent interactions and identify issues
To speed up the performance of agents

exercises.explain: Central trace logs are primarily used to track how agents communicate, which helps pinpoint issues in their interactions.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Central trace logs can only be used after an error has occurred.
exercises.explain: False. Central trace logs are useful both proactively, to analyze communication patterns, and reactively, to investigate errors.

exercises.prompt: Trace logs help us find problems in __________ communication between agents.
exercises.sentence: Trace logs help us find problems in inter-agent communication between agents.
exercises.explain: Inter-agent is the correct term that describes communication between different agents.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best describes the role of central trace logs?

options.text: They show the hardware specifications of agents.
They document interactions between agents to help debug issues.
They serve as a backup for agent data.

exercises.explain: Central trace logs document every interaction between agents, making it easier to trace back problems if an issue arises.

exercises.prompt: When investigating a communication failure in a multi-agent system, what is the first step you should take?

options.text: Look at the central trace logs to track interactions.
Ignore the problem and continue working.

exercises.explain: The first step to investigate a communication failure is to review the central trace logs. They provide information about what messages were sent, received, or missed, helping identify the source of the issue.
